forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system east of Tasmania will maintain a ridge over SA this week. A cold front will pass over the south on Saturday, weakening and clearing in the east early Sunday. A new high pressure system will develop in the Bight on Sunday, slowly drifting to the southeast on Monday while continuing to extend a ridge over SA.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
North to northeasterly 15 to 20 knots.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Mostly sunny.
Northerly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots near the Yorke Peninsula coast during the morning and early afternoon. Winds shifting south to southwesterly during the afternoon and evening.
Around 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Port Neill to Port Victoria around midday.
Becoming cloudy. 80% chance of showers south of Port Neill to Port Victoria, 50% chance elsewhere. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning south to southeasterly 15 to 20 knots during the day.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres north of Cowell to Wallaroo during the afternoon or evening.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
North to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots becoming variable below 10 knots in the middle of the day then becoming northeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
Around 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Mostly sunny.
Northerly 10 to 15 knots shifting southwesterly in the late afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Sunny morning. 60% chance of showers in the late afternoon and evening. The chance of a thunderstorm at night.
South to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning southeasterly during the evening.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Northerly 15 to 20 knots.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Mostly sunny.
Northerly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots in the west early in the morning. Winds shifting west to southwesterly below 10 knots in the late morning and early afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre around midday.
Southwesterly 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning south to southeasterly during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.
North to northeasterly 15 to 20 knots.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre around midday.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres inshore.
Mostly sunny.
Northerly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots through the Backstairs Passage during the morning and early afternoon. Winds shifting west to southwesterly in the afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Becoming cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning south to southeasterly during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
